title: Printer spooler keeps crashing after driver update

servicenow_seed.short_description: [TRAINING] Spooler crashes repeatedly after driver rollout
servicenow_seed.description: Multiple users report Print Spooler crashing every few minutes since last night's driver rollout. Restarting the spooler restores printing for ~5 minutes.

correct_steps: Verify caller, capture device names of affected users
Check related incidents - confirm pattern after the v12.4 driver rollout
Capture driver version (current and previous) and crash timestamps
Confirm spooler restart provides only short-term relief
Link affected tickets to a parent incident; escalate to Endpoint Engineering for driver rollback
rationale: Spooler crashes correlated with a fleet-wide driver rollout are a deployment regression, not per-device tickets. Escalate to the team that owns deployments.
hints: Restarting the spooler is a workaround, not a fix.
Look for a recent change. Driver versions matter.
learning_objectives: Use parent incidents for fleet-wide driver regressions

tool_clues.endpoint: Driver Print-V12.4 deployed via Intune at 02:00 last night. Crash spike began 02:30.
tool_clues.print_server: 12 endpoints reporting spoolsv.exe crash dumps in last 4 hours.
